scenario: Timer tree corruption leads to missing wakeup and system freeze
symptoms: Kernel panic - not syncing: NMI: Not continuing
INFO: task ... blocked for more than 120 seconds
rcu: INFO: rcu_sched detected stalls on CPUs/tasks

workflow.thought: Check overall system task state summary
workflow.observation: Count of tasks in each state (RU, IN, ID, PA, UN, ZO)

workflow.thought: Examine hung task detection sysctl values
workflow.observation: Number of hung task warnings issued

workflow.thought: Check hung task timeout configuration
workflow.observation: Timeout value for hung task detection

workflow.thought: Read kernel command line from memory
workflow.observation: Boot parameters including isolcpus and nohz settings

workflow.thought: List uninterruptible tasks with timestamps
workflow.observation: Recent UN tasks with PIDs and commands

workflow.thought: Analyze backtraces of all UN tasks for common patterns
workflow.action: foreach UN bt | awk '/#4 / { print $3,$5 }' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr
workflow.observation: Most frequent blocking functions in UN tasks

workflow.thought: Examine specific UN task details
workflow.observation: Task state and timing information

workflow.thought: Disassemble function to understand code flow
workflow.observation: Assembly instructions around inotify_release

workflow.thought: Check function prologue for stack usage
workflow.observation: Register pushes at function entry

workflow.thought: Find inotify_release in backtrace frames
workflow.observation: Stack frame containing inotify_release address

workflow.thought: Examine fsnotify_group structure field offset
workflow.observation: Offset of wait queue in structure

workflow.thought: Check if wait queue has pending tasks
workflow.observation: Empty or non-empty wait queue status

workflow.thought: List block devices with statistics
workflow.observation: Device major numbers, names, and request counts

workflow.thought: Check module taint status
workflow.observation: Module names and taint flags

workflow.thought: Examine another UN task details
workflow.observation: Task state and timing for different PID

workflow.thought: Set context to specific task for further analysis
workflow.observation: Task details including state and CPU

workflow.thought: Get backtrace of selected task
workflow.observation: Call stack showing blocking functions

workflow.thought: List all UN tasks in system
workflow.observation: Complete list of uninterruptible tasks

workflow.thought: Check timer tree structure for corruption
workflow.observation: Next timer pointer in clock base

workflow.thought: Identify CPU handling timer duties
workflow.observation: CPU number responsible for timer ticks

workflow.thought: Check current jiffies counter
workflow.observation: Current jiffies value for timing reference

workflow.thought: Verify NOHZ tickless mode status
workflow.observation: Boolean indicating NOHZ mode enabled

workflow.thought: Check if full NOHZ mode is running
workflow.observation: Boolean for full NOHZ operation

workflow.thought: Get NOHZ full CPU mask address
workflow.observation: Address of cpumask for NOHZ full CPUs

workflow.thought: Examine CPU mask bits
workflow.observation: Bitmask of CPUs in NOHZ full mode

workflow.thought: Get RCU nocallback CPU mask
workflow.observation: Address of cpumask for RCU nocallback CPUs

workflow.thought: Examine RCU nocallback mask bits
workflow.observation: Bitmask of CPUs with RCU callbacks disabled

workflow.thought: Calculate complement of mask for analysis
workflow.observation: Bits representing non-isolated CPUs

workflow.thought: Examine specific bit in NOHZ mask
workflow.observation: First 64-bit word of NOHZ mask

workflow.thought: Check housekeeping CPU mask
workflow.observation: First 64-bit word of housekeeping mask

workflow.thought: Verify NOHZ mask value again
workflow.observation: Consistency check of NOHZ mask

workflow.thought: Examine run queues for specific CPUs
workflow.observation: Tasks currently queued on CPUs 36 and 37

workflow.thought: List timers for specific CPUs in detail
workflow.observation: All timers with expiration times and functions

workflow.thought: Calculate time difference between current and expiration
workflow.observation: Time delta in seconds showing overdue timers

workflow.thought: Convert seconds to hours for readability
workflow.observation: Hours since timer should have expired

workflow.thought: Repeat timer examination for confirmation
workflow.observation: Consistent timer state showing scheduling stall

root_cause_analysis: The commit below will resolve the issue.
lib/timerqueue: Rely on rbtree semantics for next timer

Simplify the timerqueue code by using cached rbtrees and rely on the tree leftmost node semantics to get the timer with earliest expiration time. This is a drop in conversion, and therefore semantics remain untouched.

The runtime overhead of cached rbtrees is be pretty much the same as the current head->next method, noting that when removing the leftmost node, a common operation for the timerqueue, the rb_next(leftmost) is O(1) as well, so the next timer will either be the right node or its parent. Therefore no extra pointer chasing. Finally, the size of the struct timerqueue_head remains the same.
